title: RepairProposal v1alpha1
description: A schema-constrained desired-state change proposal tied to a Report fingerprint (docs/architecture/reporting.md §11.3 omca_propose: 'Accepts a schema-constrained desired-state proposal tied to a report fingerprint. It does not accept raw native file edits.'). init.md decision 12: any LLM may submit a repair proposal, but only the deterministic core writes anything. docs/product/requirements.md §8 (LLM Boundary) and FR-10: OMCA validates every proposal against schemas, capability gates, policy, ownership, source digests, and risk confirmation before writing anything. docs/project/roadmap.md M4 additionally requires validating against report fingerprint, schemas, capability, ownership, policy, and risk.

reportFingerprint.description: Must match the spec.fingerprint of the Report this proposal was computed against; a stale fingerprint is rejected rather than reinterpreted.

model.description: Required when kind is llm (runtime model ID, docs/architecture/reporting.md §12).

rationale.description: LLM or human explanation. Kept structurally separate from the deterministic fields above: docs/architecture/reporting.md §12, 'An annotation cannot change a Drift category, evidence level, capability, guarantee, or source disposition.'

ownership.description: The ownership tier of the artifacts this proposal would touch; validated against the target's actual ownership before compilation (docs/project/roadmap.md M4).

changes.description: One or more targeted desired-state patches (Profile/Binding/Activation), never a raw native file edit.

patch.description: A partial document merged into the target using that document's own merge semantics; opaque to this schema.

confirmation.description: Derived from the risk-based confirmation table in docs/product/requirements.md §7 (not a verbatim doc enum name — that table lists actions, this closes them into a checkable vocabulary). A PROHIBITED proposal must be rejected before staging, never merely flagged.
